name: project_aura
description: ESP32‑S3 air‑quality station with LVGL UI, MQTT, Home Assistant
readme_summary: Project Aura AQ is an open-source ESP32-S3 based air quality station featuring a touch-friendly LVGL UI, local web setup, and MQTT integration for Home Assistant. It provides comprehensive air quality telemetry and is designed for easy assembly by makers.
key_features: Professional telemetry: PM0.5/PM1/PM2.5/PM4/PM10, CO, CO2, VOC, NOx, temperature, humidity, absolute humidity (AH), pressure, HCHO.
No soldering required: designed for easy assembly using Grove/QT connectors.
Smooth LVGL UI with night mode, custom themes, and status indicators.
Integrated web dashboard with live state, charts, events, settings sync, DAC page, and OTA firmware update.
Easy setup: Wi-Fi AP onboarding + mDNS access.
Home Assistant ready: automatic MQTT discovery.
Optional DAC control (GP8403, 0-10V): manual levels/timer plus automatic demand mode.
Robust Safe Boot: automatic rollback to the last-known-good config after crashes.
primary_use_case: To build a DIY, ESP32-S3 powered air quality monitoring station with a graphical user interface and smart home integration.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, transparent sphere representing the air in a room, with visible streams of different colored particles flowing into it, symbolizing various air pollutants like PM2.5, CO2, and VOCs. Inside the sphere, a small, sleek ESP32-S3 device with a glowing LVGL interface is actively analyzing these particles. Thin, glowing data lines connect the device to a subtle, futuristic Home Assistant dashboard interface visible in the background, showing clear readings and charts. The overall mood is clean, informative, and slightly futuristic, with a focus on making the invisible air quality data visible and understandable. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with soft, ambient lighting and a color palette emphasizing blues, greens, and subtle reds for alerts.

name: rdn
readme_summary: rdn is a terminal-based file manager written in Rust, inspired by the classic Dos Navigator. It offers a two-panel interface, essential file operations, and a retro DOS-accurate color theme, aiming to bring a familiar orthodox file manager experience to modern terminals.
primary_use_case: A modern, retro-inspired terminal file manager for efficient file navigation and operations.
cover_image_prompt: A vintage, glowing computer terminal screen displaying a dual-pane file system interface, reminiscent of old DOS applications. On the left pane, a user is actively selecting files with a cyan cursor, while the right pane shows files being copied as digital particles. Subtle elements like a small Tetris game window in a corner and a 'rdn' logo integrated into the terminal's bezel connect it to software. The overall mood is nostalgic yet functional, with a dark background and vibrant, retro-futuristic lighting. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clean lines and clear visual metaphors for file management.

name: ai-sdk-tools
description: Essential utilities for building production-ready AI applications with Vercel AI SDK. State management, debugging, structured streaming, intelligent agents, and caching.
readme_summary: This TypeScript package provides essential utilities for building production-ready AI applications using the Vercel AI SDK. It offers solutions for state management, debugging, structured streaming, intelligent agents, caching, and persistent memory, aiming to streamline AI development.
key_features: Development tools for debugging AI applications
Stream structured, type-safe artifacts from AI tools
Universal caching for AI SDK tools
Persistent memory system for AI agents
primary_use_case: To provide developers with a suite of tools and utilities to enhance the development, debugging, and deployment of AI applications built with the Vercel AI SDK.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled architect meticulously constructing a complex, futuristic city skyline using glowing, interconnected building blocks that represent AI components. The architect is shown directing streams of data (visualized as light particles) from various sources (like a cloud and a server rack) into the city's core, which then distributes them efficiently. Small, transparent screens hover around the scene, displaying snippets of code and AI agent interactions. The overall mood is one of organized creation and intelligent automation. The style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant, futuristic color palette, emphasizing the seamless integration and robust structure provided by the 'ai-sdk-tools'.

name: Nano-PDF
description: Edit PDF files with Nano Banana
readme_summary: Nano PDF Editor is a Python-based command-line tool that allows users to edit PDF documents using natural language prompts. It leverages Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image model to understand and execute edits, preserving the original text layer through OCR re-hydration.
primary_use_case: Editing PDF documents, including text, images, and slide content, through intuitive natural language commands.
cover_image_prompt: A magical quill pen, labeled 'Nano PDF', is hovering over an open PDF document. The quill is actively rewriting text and rearranging elements on the page based on spoken words emanating from a nearby speech bubble containing simple text prompts like 'Update title' and 'Add logo'. The PDF pages transform from a dull, static state to vibrant, dynamic content. Subtle digital particles and glowing lines connect the quill to the PDF, symbolizing AI-powered editing. The background is a clean, modern workspace with a hint of code on a small screen. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, inviting color palette.

name: iothackbot
description: IoT HackBot: A collection of Claude Skills and custom tooling for hybrid IoT pentesting
readme_summary: IoTHackBot is an open-source toolkit for IoT security testing, offering a suite of command-line tools and AI-assisted Claude Code skills for discovering vulnerabilities in devices, firmware, and applications. It facilitates network reconnaissance, device-specific testing, firmware analysis, and Android app examination.
key_features: Network Discovery & Reconnaissance (wsdiscovery, iotnet, netflows, nmap)
AI-assisted workflows with Claude Code skills
primary_use_case: Comprehensive security testing and vulnerability assessment of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and embedded systems.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled digital locksmith, wearing a futuristic headset, is meticulously examining a complex, multi-layered padlock that represents an IoT device. The locksmith uses a glowing toolkit, with small holographic screens displaying code snippets and network traffic visualizations, to identify and bypass security flaws. One screen shows a 'vulnerability found' alert, while another displays a successful 'access granted' message. The background is a dimly lit, abstract network of interconnected circuits and data streams, with the repository name 'IoTHackBot' subtly integrated into the environment. The overall mood is one of focused investigation and digital exploration. Rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a dark, high-tech color palette and glowing accents.
